stories.seo_title: Kittens purr to self-soothe — it's not just cute 🐱
stories.yt_description:
Kittens are known for their adorable purrs. But what's really surprising is that kittens purr to self-soothe and even heal their own bones. Source: Pexels
#Shorts #Animals #Cats
stories.hook: Kittens purr to heal their own bones
stories.script: Kittens purr to heal their own bones. They also purr when they're happy or relaxed. Kittens have unique nose prints, just like human fingerprints. Their little meows are actually a form of communication with their mothers. What's the most surprising thing you've learned about kittens?

stories.hook: Cats purr to heal their own bones
stories.script: Cats purr to heal their own bones. They can purr when they're happy, scared, or even giving birth. Purring helps them relax and reduces swelling. Cats also have scent glands on their faces and near their whiskers. What's the strangest thing you've seen a cat do?
